body:
On https://github.com/libevent/libevent/tree/release-2.1.5-beta, I am trying to use `evhttp_set_bevcb` to set up a bufferevent filter.
Doing so results in the filter callbacks not being called.
The http callbacks are not being called either - events just get stuck/disappear somewhere.
When not inserting the filter (i.e. just returning a plain socket bufferevent), `evhttp_set_bevcb` appears to work however.

Code: https://gist.github.com/LudwigOrtmann/ff255b1dcbfe53bbc934

comments.body:
@ellzey Thank you for the quick response!
An initial test worked well on my side too.

Hm, there's more problems:
Data doesn't make it through to the `output_filter` while the `input_filter` works fine. It's the same when setting the `output_filter` to `NULL`.
Also: `NULL` as an `input_filter` does not work, i.e. no data passes through.

I pushed the changes (payload in reply, `evbuffer_remove_buffer`) to the gist.

Interestingly, in this reduced example the application just crashes instead of "nothing happens" on the output path.
Getting on a plane right now, will debug during my 7 hour flight. Thanks for waiting.

Can you try out the latest master branch for the fix? (works for me). remember you need to `evbuffer_remove_buffer(src, dst, evbuffer_get_length(src));`  for both of your in and out filters to avoid an infinite loop.

Now, the [test code](https://gist.github.com/LudwigOrtmann/ff255b1dcbfe53bbc934) does not crash anymore but still does not work.

When opening a connection, libevent hangs, apparently waiting for a socket to become ready:

```
)                 = 7
write(1, "filter activated\n", 17filter activated
)      = 17
accept4(6, 0x7fff3891c880, [128], SOCK_CLOEXEC|SOCK_NONBLOCK) = -1 EAGAIN (Resource temporarily unavailable)
epoll_wait(3, 
```

BTW:
The same (waiting for socket to become ready) also happens with my own libevent based http + filter implementation which worked with the pre-patch version of libevent.

This is a pre-patch strace of the relevant passage in my own http + filter implementation:

```
{{EPOLLIN, {u32=7, u64=7}}}, 32, -1) = 1
accept4(7, {sa_family=AF_INET6, sin6_port=htons(34614), inet_pton(AF_INET6, "::1", &sin6_addr), sin6_flowinfo=0, sin6_scope_id=0}, [28], SOCK_NONBLOCK) = 14
write(1, "bev_cb\n", 7bev_cb
)                 = 7
write(1, "created bev: 0x161cfb0\n", 23created bev: 0x161cfb0
) = 23
write(1, "created fbev: 0x161f6b0\n", 24created fbev: 0x161f6b0
) = 24
epoll_ctl(3, EPOLL_CTL_ADD, 14, {EPOLLOUT, {u32=14, u64=14}}) = 0
epoll_ctl(3, EPOLL_CTL_MOD, 14, {EPOLLIN|EPOLLOUT, {u32=14, u64=14}}) = 0
accept4(7, 0x7fffa1d2dfc0, [128], SOCK_NONBLOCK) = -1 EAGAIN (Resource temporarily unavailable)
epoll_wait(3, {{EPOLLIN|EPOLLOUT, {u32=14, u64=14}}}, 32, -1) = 1
ioctl(14, FIONREAD, [83])               = 0
readv(14, [{"GET /fooo HTTP/1.1\r\nHost: local"..., 83}], 1) = 83
```
Ah, I was confused, sorry, I did not test the master branch but the release tag...
On master my test as well as my own http implementation are working.

OK, from my point of view the bug is fixed.
The release tagged version still contains it, so I'm not clicking the "close" button...

Thank you for tending to this issue!
I'm closing this issue since it's technically fixed; just have to wait for next release (I dislike having fixed things in the issues queue). I will notify you for next beta..

title: http + filter isn't working as expected (release-2.1.5-beta)
